request.question: During the 2007 Oakland Athletics season, Ken Macha was replaced by a manager who now is the bench coach of what team?

title: 2017 Oakland Athletics season
2007 Oakland Athletics season
Don Wakamatsu
Bob Geren
Steve Staggs
1978 Oakland Athletics season
Rob Thomson
2014 Oakland Athletics season
Roger Smithberg
1980 Oakland Athletics season
sentences: The 2017 Oakland Athletics season was the 50th for the Oakland Athletics franchise in Oakland (all at Oakland Coliseum), as well as the 117th in club history.
The A's began the season on April 3 at home against Los Angeles Angels of Anaheim.
They will finish the season at the Texas Rangers on October 1.
The Oakland Athletics' 2007 season was their first since 2004 in which they defended a title.
The A's were without Frank Thomas (Toronto) and ace Barry Zito (San Francisco), both players who the A's lost to free agency.
They filled the space left by Frank Thomas by signing free agent catcher Mike Piazza to a one-year contract worth $8.5 million to be the team's designated hitter.

They were also without Ken Macha, their former manager who was fired after 4 seasons with the team and replaced by Bob Geren, as well as third-base coach Ron Washington, who accepted the managerial position of the Texas Rangers.
It was their first losing season since 1998.
Wilbur Donald Wakamatsu (born February 22, 1963) is an American former professional baseball player, scout, manager and current coach.
He played as a catcher in Major League Baseball for the Chicago White Sox.
He was hired as bench coach of the Kansas City Royals for the 2014 season.
He was the manager of the Seattle Mariners for the season, as well as the majority of the season.
He was the Toronto Blue Jays' bench coach for 2011 and 2012, after which he was replaced by DeMarlo Hale.
During the 2013 season he worked as a scout for the New York Yankees in the Dallas/Fort Worth area.
Robert Peter Geren (born September 22, 1961) is a former Major League Baseball catcher and manager.
During a 5-year playing career, he played for the New York Yankees (1988–1991) and the San Diego Padres (1993).
He managed the Oakland Athletics from 2007 through 2011 and also coached for the New York Mets.
He is the current bench coach of the Los Angeles Dodgers.
Stephen Robert Staggs (born May 6, 1951) is a former Major League Baseball second baseman who played for two seasons.
He played 72 games for the Toronto Blue Jays during the 1977 Toronto Blue Jays season and 47 games for the Oakland Athletics during the 1978 Oakland Athletics season.
The 1978 Oakland Athletics season was the team's eleventh in Oakland, California.
The team sought to rebound from its first losing season in a decade (a 63-99 result in 1977).
Despite low expectations, the Athletics remained competitive for nearly three-quarters of the season.
Despite posting a respectable 61-56 mark through 117 games, the Athletics collapsed in the season's final weeks; their 8-37 finish ensured a second consecutive season of fewer than 70 wins.
Robert Lewis Thomson (born August 16, 1963) is a Canadian bench coach for the New York Yankees.
He previously served as bench coach during the 2008 season, third base coach from 2009 - 2014, and returned to serving as bench coach in 2015.
He has also served as Major League Field Coordinator for the organization.
The 2014 Oakland Athletics season was the 46th for the franchise at O.co Coliseum, as well as the 114th in club history.
The Athletics entered the season hoping to win a third consecutive American League West championship; to that end, the team made a number of key signings and trades during the 2013-14 MLB offseason.
Notably, Athletics traded outfielder Michael Choice for left fielder Craig Gentry and pitcher Josh Lindblom; they also traded the promising but oft-injured Brett Anderson for reliever Drew Pomeranz.
Additional trades brought in relievers Fernando Abad (acquired for John Wooten), Luke Gregerson (acquired for Seth Smith), and Jim Johnson (acquired for Jemile Weeks and David Freitas).
In free agency, the Athletics signed former All-Star starting pitcher Scott Kazmir to a two-year deal.
These moves, among others, sought to bolster the depth of team's starting pitching and bullpen.
Roger Craig Smithberg (born March 21, 1966) is a former Major League Baseball pitcher who played for two seasons.
He pitched for the Oakland Athletics for 13 games during the 1993 Oakland Athletics season and two games during the 1994 Oakland Athletics season.
The 1980 Oakland Athletics season was the team's thirteenth season in Oakland.
The A's, under first-year manager Billy Martin, began the season with low expectations following their insipid 1979 campaign.

Strong performances from pitchers Mike Norris, Matt Keough, and Rick Langford, along with the brilliant play of breakout star (and future Hall-of-Famer) Rickey Henderson, paved the way for a staggering 29-win increase over the previous year's output.
The Athletics, only one year removed from baseball's worst record, swung to a second-place finish behind their 83-79 record.

candidate.summarize1.predict: Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict: Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.
candidate.summarize2.predict: Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2`, produce the field `answer` as follows. Use only the visible summaries (summary_1 and summary_2) as the evidential basis for the final answer; do not add external facts except where the instructions below explicitly allow a limited, well‑justified use of internal background knowledge. Always produce the final result inside the [[ ## answer ## ]] block and make that block contain only the minimal answer string required by these rules.

1) General minimal-answer rule
- If the summaries unambiguously support a single short answer (a short name, title, demonym, year, or single noun/phrase of roughly 1–5 words), emit only that minimal token sequence as the answer. Normalize the chosen substring by removing any leading/trailing whitespace or surrounding punctuation but preserve the phrase's internal characters and capitalization exactly as shown in the summaries. Do not add quotes, em dashes, braces, labels, trailing punctuation, explanatory clauses, or any additional text.

2) When both summaries agree on an identical short phrase
- If summary_1 and summary_2 contain the same short named-entity string (after lowercasing only for agreement detection), extract that common phrase, strip surrounding punctuation/whitespace, and emit it verbatim (preserving original capitalization) as the sole content of [[ ## answer ## ]] with no surrounding or appended characters.

3) Patterned question triggers (minimal formatting + deterministic selection)
- 'Which ... is from X, A or B?': When the question asks which of two named options is from a place and the summaries clearly indicate a single band/person as the answer, emit only the minimal band/person name supported by the summaries.
- Comparative membership questions (e.g., 'Does A or B have more band members?'): If the summaries clearly indicate which band has more members, emit only that band's name.
- Short common-name questions (e.g., 'What do people in the UK commonly call the location ...?'): If both summaries provide the same short common name, emit only that short name string.

4) Nationality questions
- If the question asks for a nationality/demonym and either summary_1 or summary_2 contains a demonym, emit only the minimal demonym token/phrase. Prefer the first demonym found in summary_1; if none appears in summary_1, use the first found in summary_2. Emit it verbatim (no added words or punctuation).

5) Two-performer composition specificity
- If the question asks for the "type of musical composition" used by a named act and both summaries use a generic word like 'song(s)', you may consult internal knowledge only to determine whether the act is a two-person (duo) group. If you can reliably confirm the act is a two-person group, replace the generic term with the single-word or very short noun that names a composition intended for two performers (for example, 'duet') and emit that single word as the minimal answer. If you cannot reliably confirm duo membership, emit the minimal noun exactly as it appears in the summaries.

6) Narrow assumed referent (single-name trigger)
- If the question supplies only a given name (e.g., 'Maurice') and the visible summaries explicitly mention a specific example with that given name (e.g., a named example appears in the summaries) and the summaries identify the requested fact (e.g., the award), then you may assume that referent and emit the minimal answer (for example, a single year) if you have sufficient internal confidence. If you do not have sufficient internal confidence, follow the normal clarification behavior (emit a brief clarifying question as the answer field) rather than guessing.

7) What to avoid (formatting and content prohibitions)
- Never add explanatory text, full sentences, comparisons, justifications, or provenance in the answer field. Do not append decorative punctuation (em dashes, extra commas, trailing periods), quotes, braces, JSON-like structures, leading newlines, or any characters not part of the normalized minimal phrase. Do not invent facts not present in the summaries except where the two narrowly scoped rules above permit limited internal confirmation (duo-confirmation and the single-name assumed referent). If the summaries do not unambiguously support a single minimal answer and you lack permitted internal justification, emit a concise clarification request as the answer.

Output contract reminder: The produced `answer` must be exactly the minimal answer string determined by the rules above (or a short clarification question when required). The [[ ## answer ## ]] block must contain only that string and nothing else.

response.hop1_query: During the 2007 Oakland Athletics season, Ken Macha was replaced by a manager who now is the bench coach of what team?
hop1_docs: 2007 Oakland Athletics season | The Oakland Athletics' 2007 season was their first since 2004 in which they defended a title.  The A's were without Frank Thomas (Toronto) and ace Barry Zito (San Francisco), both players who the A's lost to free agency.  They filled the space left by Frank Thomas by signing free agent catcher Mike Piazza to a one-year contract worth $8.5 million to be the team's designated hitter.  They were also without Ken Macha, their former manager who was fired after 4 seasons with the team and replaced by Bob Geren, as well as third-base coach Ron Washington, who accepted the managerial position of the Texas Rangers.  It was their first losing season since 1998.

Ken Macha | Kenneth Edward Macha ( ; born September 29, 1950, in Monroeville, Pennsylvania) is a former Major League Baseball third baseman and manager.  He managed the Oakland Athletics from 2003–2006, including American League Western Division championships in both his first and final seasons with the team, and later managed the Milwaukee Brewers (2009–10).

Bob Geren | Robert Peter Geren (born September 22, 1961) is a former Major League Baseball catcher and manager.  During a 5-year playing career, he played for the New York Yankees (1988–1991) and the San Diego Padres (1993).  He managed the Oakland Athletics from 2007 through 2011 and also coached for the New York Mets.  He is the current bench coach of the Los Angeles Dodgers.

Vern Hoscheit | Vernard Arthur Hoscheit (April 1, 1922 – June 11, 2007) was an American professional baseball catcher, coach and manager.  He served as a coach on four World Series championship Major League Baseball teams with the Oakland Athletics and the New York Mets.  Hoscheit was the Mets' bullpen coach from 1984 to 1987, which included their World Series run in 1986.  During that season's spring training, Hoscheit accurately predicted that the team would clinch the National League East Division title on September 17, long before any regular season game was even played.

2010 Arizona Diamondbacks season | The Arizona Diamondbacks' 2010 season, the franchise's 13th season in Major League Baseball, included the team's attempt to make the playoffs for the first time since 2007.  On July 1, 2010, the Arizona Diamondbacks fired Manager A. J. Hinch following a 31-48 start to the 2010 season and promoted Kirk Gibson who had served as the bench coach of the team since the 2007 season.  Along with the dismissal of Hinch came the firing of General Manager Josh Byrnes.

List of Oakland Athletics managers | The Oakland Athletics are a professional baseball team based in Oakland, California.  Before moving to Oakland in 1968, the team played in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania from 1901 through 1954 and in Kansas City, Missouri from 1955 through 1967.  The Athletics are members of the American League (AL) West division in Major League Baseball (MLB).  In baseball, the head coach of a team is called the manager, or more formally, the field manager.  The duties of the team manager include team strategy and leadership on and off the field.  The team has employed 30 different managers in its history.  The current Athletics' manager is Bob Melvin.

Don Wakamatsu | Wilbur Donald Wakamatsu (born February 22, 1963) is an American former professional baseball player, scout, manager and current coach.  He played as a catcher in Major League Baseball for the Chicago White Sox.  He was hired as bench coach of the Kansas City Royals for the 2014 season.  He was the manager of the Seattle Mariners for the season, as well as the majority of the season.  He was the Toronto Blue Jays' bench coach for 2011 and 2012, after which he was replaced by DeMarlo Hale.  During the 2013 season he worked as a scout for the New York Yankees in the Dallas/Fort Worth area.

Mark Kotsay | Mark Steven Kotsay (born December 2, 1975) is an American professional baseball coach and a former outfielder.  In 2016 , he served as the bench coach for the Oakland Athletics.

Oakland Golden Grizzlies baseball | The Oakland Golden Grizzlies baseball team is a varsity intercollegiate athletic team of Oakland University in Rochester, Michigan, United States.  The team is a member of the Horizon League, which is part of the National Collegiate Athletic Association's Division I.  The team plays its home games at Oakland University Baseball Field in Rochester, Michigan.  The Golden Grizzlies are coached by co-head coaches Jacke Healey and Colin Kaline.  They were named as co-head coaches in July 2016.

Tom Melvin | Tom Melvin (born October 1, 1961) is an American football coach who is the current tight ends coach for the Kansas City Chiefs of the National Football League (NFL).  His cousin, Bob Melvin, is manager of the Oakland Athletics.
